calendar.matters:
Petition For Assignment Order Pursuant To Probate Code Section 1000 And Code Of Civil Procedure Section 708.510
Off Calendar Nov-01-2022 Off Calendar

Ex Parte Application And Supporting Declaration Of Michael B. Bassi For Temporary Restraining Order Pending Hearing On Petition For Assignment Order And To Advance Hearing Date On Petition For Assignment Order
Off Calendar Jul-06-2022 Denied

Motion To Set Aside Order, And For Entry Of Statement Of Decision Before Re Entry Of Order On Petition For Allowance Of Compensation To Former Trustee And Counsel
Off Calendar Feb-03-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Green appeared in person for and with Suzanne Farnocchia (by zoom) and for Martha Eggeling.
Michael Bassi appeared by zoom for John Harms (former trustee).

The parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not they had oral argument at the hearing that was under submission, whether Mr. Bassi having provided a proposed order to the court in advance of the hearing without providing a copy to Mr. Green was ex parte communication, the capacity (trustee or individual) at which Mr. Bassi was representing Mr. Harms, whether there was a waiver of trustee compensation on behalf of Mr. Harms, the trusts (3 Harms Trusts/3 Eggeling Trusts) being identical and all expenses having been divided equally, Mr. Harm having not charged fees for seven years and not keeping time records, whether Mr. Green is now requesting a new trial, the issue of whether or not Mr. Green is entitled to a statement of decision as there was no trial, etc. The court noted that at the hearing both counsel indicated that the matter was adequately briefed and that they did not wish to have an evidentiary hearing, or a trial, and that the threshold issues that were discussed were based on its review of the file and what is saw as a factual dispute between the parties. The court also spoke of what it means when parties come to court and say the word "submitted", stated that the amounts to be awarded were left blank in the proposed order that was provided by Mr. Bassi, that it read everything again and that it filled out the order accordingly with the assistance of its staff. The court confirms that it is granting Mr. Bassi's request for judicial notice as described in its tentative ruling. The court confirms its' tentative ruling. The Motion to Set Aside Order, and for Entry of Statement of Decision Before Re-entry of Order on Petition for Allowance of Compensation to Former Trustee and Counsel is denied. A copy of the proposed order was provided to Mr. Green by court staff for his review.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Xiao. Reporter: Alesia Collins, CSR #7751, 415-591-3333, alesiacsr@yahoo.com

Petition For Allowance Of Compensation To Former Trustee And To Reimburse Former Trustee For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Oct-18-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Green appeared in person for Suzanne Farnocchia.
Michael Bassi appeared by zoom for John Harms (Petitioner).

The court stated that it is not in a position to review and adjudicate Mr. Harm's fee request right now because there are too many threshold issues that need to be resolved. The court also questioned if Mr. Harms provided the accountings to Ms. Farnocchia, and asked about status of the assets and if Ms. Farnocchia intends to file a new petition to renew her request for any relief against Mr. Harms upon her review of the items provided. In addition, the court asked if either party intends to file any motion to request the Court's ruling on the issue of whether or not Mr. Bassi's fees should be recoverable from the trust, whether Mr. Harms' fees should be denied based on estoppel, waiver, or any other doctrine and whether there are terms of the court-approved stipulation between the parties that need to be enforced under CCP 664.6. Mr. Green stated that there is no accounting, that the assets have been turned over, that the issue of whether Ms. Farnocchia will be filing a petition is under consideration and that the stipulation resolved a great deal of the matters. Mr. Bassi stated that he thought the briefing of the parties addressed the issues fairly thoroughly. Mr. Green stated that he believes it was briefed thoroughly except for a summary. Both counsel indicated that no one is requesting an evidentiary hearing or a trial. The Petition for Allowance of Compensation to Former Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: S. Park. Not reported.

Petition For Allowance Of Compensation To Former Trustee And To Reimburse Former Trustee For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Costs

Petition For Order Approving Stipulation Of Parties, Reservation Of Limited Issues, And Retention Of Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Dec-12-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Green for and with Suzanne Farnocchia (Petitioner).
Michael Bassi for and with John Harms (Respondent).

The Court noted that what is being referred to as a stipulation appears to be a settlement agreement and that it does not have a formal consent by the Successor Trustee. Mr. Green stated that he has the consent, that it is not filed and that a proposed order with the agreement attached to it was provided yesterday. Mr. Bassi stated that his Client does not like the stipulation, but he is not objecting and is willing to proceed under the terms of the stipulation. The Court granted the Petition for Order Approving Stipulation of Parties, etc. (subject to review of the order). The Petition to Require Trustee to Provide Copies of the Terms of Any and All Trusts, etc. is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Siu. Not reported.

Petition To Require Trustee To Provide Copies Of The Terms Of Any And All Trusts; To Provide A Complete Accounting; To Determine The Validity Of Any Such Trust; And/Or The Existence Of Same; To Direct The Combination Or Division Of Trusts; And/Or Termination And Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-14-2019 Continued To Oct-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: James P. Green for Suzanne C. Farnocchia and Martha L. Eggeling. E. Neal McGettgan for John Harms (Trustor).

The Court stated that it understood that the parties reached an agreement on the bulk of the issues, that it checked the Court's website this morning, that the Petition for Order Approving Stipulation of Parties was rejected, and that there would need to be a correction by counsel with regard to the rejected filing. Mr. Green requested that the Court continue the matter to allow time for the correction to be made and the petition to be filed. Mr. Green also stated that if/when the petition is approved that there would be one or two remaining issues for the Court to decide. Mr. Harm stated that his client would be retaining other counsel to represent him with regard to the remaining issues and that he would like to make sure that a continuance would give his client adequate time to retain new counsel. The Court then continued the Petition to Require Trust to Provide Copies of the Terms of Any and All Trusts; to Provide a Complete Accounting, etc., to October 23, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.
